topic: IPL cricket — who's going to win

turns.content: IPL mega auction results are out. what do you reckon about the squads this year

The mega auction always shakes things up -- some teams rebuild completely and others double down on their core. Which team do you follow? And is there a particular squad you think has done well or badly in the auction?

Mumbai Indians mate. always have been always will be. rohit is still there which is the main thing but theyve let go of some players I really liked. trying to stay positive though cos the new picks look decent on paper

Mumbai Indians in a rebuild phase is always interesting because they've got the money and the scouting network to pull it off. Rohit staying is huge -- he's the identity of that franchise at this point. Mega auctions usually hurt squads with strong cores because they have to break up what works, but MI have reinvented themselves before.

Which new picks are you most excited about? And who did they let go that's hurt the most?

losing Suryakumar stung the most if im honest. the man is box office innit he just plays shots nobody else would even think of. but I get it the price was mad and they needed to balance the squad. theyve picked up a couple of good young Indian batters though so hopefully one of them steps up

Losing Suryakumar is painful -- he's one of the most watchable batters in T20 cricket. Those 360-degree shots are unreal. But you're right, mega auctions force tough calls and sometimes you have to let a star go to build a balanced squad rather than blow the budget on one player.

Young Indian batters are the smart investment. The IPL always produces breakout stars who go from unknown to international player in one tournament. MI have a history of spotting them too -- Hardik Pandya, Bumrah, Ishan Kishan all came through their system.

Who do you think wins it this year? Is MI your prediction or your hope?

haha both innit. im saying MI but I know thats my heart not my head. if im being realistic its probably CSK or RCB this year. CSK cos they just never go away and RCB cos theyve finally built a proper bowling attack for once

CSK defying logic is practically a tradition at this point. Every year people write them off and every year they're in the top four. And RCB with a bowling attack is a scary thought -- they've had the batting firepower for years, if the bowling actually shows up they could finally win one.

What about KKR? They've been strong the last couple of seasons. And Gujarat Titans seem to have built smartly again.

yeah KKR are dark horses for sure. Shreyas Iyer as captain is solid he reads the game well. Gujarat I'm not so sure about this time, I think they lost a bit too much in the auction. Rajasthan Royals could be good though Sanju Samson is class when hes in the mood. problem with Rajasthan is consistency innit they blow hot and cold

That's Rajasthan's eternal problem -- on their day they can beat anyone, but you never know which version is turning up. Samson is brilliant but frustrating in equal measure. He'll play a 30-ball 80 one game and get out for 4 the next.

Sounds like your top four prediction would be something like CSK, RCB, KKR, and MI with a bit of hope. Is that about right?

yeah id go with that. CSK RCB KKR and then MI sneaking in fourth cos its written in the stars mate. honestly though I just love watching the IPL. staying up late with a brew watching cricket is the best part of spring for me. the time difference is annoying though half the games dont start til half 3 UK time

Half three start means you're watching until half seven or eight in the evening, which isn't too bad for the afternoon games. It's the late games that start at 8pm India time -- half two UK -- those are the ones during the working day that you miss.

At least with the evening games you can catch them after work. Nothing wrong with settling in for a couple of hours of cricket with a brew. How do you watch it -- is it on Sky or somewhere else these days?

nah I stream it online. the afternoon games I sometimes have on in the van between jobs if im being honest haha. just the audio obviously im not watching telly while driving. Arjun is starting to get into it too which is brilliant, he watches the highlights with me

Audio in the van between jobs is a perfectly reasonable way to follow a match -- half the country does the same with Test matches on TMS. And getting Arjun into cricket is great. IPL is perfect for kids because it's fast, exciting, and over in a few hours. None of the patience required for a five-day Test.

Does he have a favourite player yet, or is he just enjoying the big hitting?

hes all about the big hitting at the moment. every time someone smacks a six he goes mental. hes starting to like Hardik Pandya actually cos he bowls and bats and fields which Arjun thinks is the coolest thing. to be fair to the kid he picked a good one there Pandya is class

Pandya is an excellent choice -- an all-rounder who can hit sixes, bowl fast, and take athletic catches is basically a superhero to a cricket-mad kid. And he's got the swagger too, which kids love. If Arjun starts wanting to bowl fast AND bat, you might have an all-rounder on your hands as well as a striker.

Right, so the prediction stands: CSK, RCB, KKR, MI in the top four, with Mumbai going all the way because destiny demands it. Roll on March for the first matches.
destiny demands it haha I love that. right mate ive got to go, got a job to quote in Harborne. cheers for the cricket chat needed a break from thinking about boilers and pipes. come on MI
Good luck with the quote in Harborne. Enjoy the IPL when it kicks off and I hope Mumbai Indians give you something to celebrate this year. Come on MI. Cheers!
